what_it_does: It is a posted noticeboard note by Tim that comments on a prior item about hierarchical block selection, expands on the meaning of block as a recurring concept, and adds a meta-observation that surrounding tooling, workbench vaults, and substrate MCP artifacts are self-generated and only partially functional.
observations: The file is a note with YAML frontmatter and a Markdown body, linked as a comment on board://item-41f397a0.

It restates a block-selection gesture: long-press to activate selection, thumb up for coarser granularity, down for finer, starting at the tap point, with hierarchy word < sentence < paragraph < under-a-heading < whole.
It says space/zone regions after paragraphs and between bullets are selectable, and left/right are reserved.
It claims block is an overloaded term that appears across many experiments.
It mentions two main places for block selection on screen, one already referenced and another called the workbench, which has a repository and a vault.
It says the vault is one of those registered in the substrate MCP that Claude Code has, roughly a dozen in total.
It asserts that the MCP (referred to as MCB) was produced in the same way as everything else.
It claims everything not from a recognized provider was built by the author, who never read, wrote, or reviewed the code, and that it does not fully work but works enough to be beneficial.
standouts: The strong meta-claim that self-generated code and tooling are only pragmatically functional, not fully working.
The explicit link between a UI selection gesture and backend concepts such as the workbench, vault, and substrate MCP.
The repeated emphasis on block as a cross-cutting, overloaded concept.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses an informal, spoken style with run-on sentences and ambiguous pronouns.
Mixes a concrete UI specification recap with broad architectural and provenance assertions.
concerns: Assertions about code authorship, generation process, and functionality are stated without supporting evidence or links.
Terms such as MCB, recognized provider, workbench, and vault are used without definition in this file.
The note conflates a user-interface selection concept with backend tooling in a way that may confuse scope.
The claim that nothing works fully could undermine trust but is not substantiated here.
notes: Tim's stream-of-consciousness note connecting a hierarchical block-selection gesture to broader block-based tooling and warning that self-generated artifacts are only partially functional.
questions: What exactly is the workbench and how do its repository and vault relate to block selection?
What does MCB stand for and what is its precise role?
Which previously mentioned place for block selection is being referenced?
What is meant by a recognized provider in this context?
Which specific vaults are registered in the substrate MCP and how many are there?
How does the author distinguish between works enough and does not work?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a comment on a block-selection specification, and the file does that while also serving as a broader meta-commentary on self-generated tooling; both purposes are present.
apparent_themes: Block-based content abstraction spanning UI and system layers
intention_signals: Tim seems to be tying a concrete UI interaction idea to a larger architectural notion of blocks and simultaneously documenting his view that almost all surrounding tooling is self-created, unreviewed, and only good enough to be useful.
novelty: The unusual combination of a detailed gesture-specification comment with a sweeping claim about the authorship and reliability of the entire self-generated codebase.
contribution: It preserves a first-person record that connects the block-selection gesture to recurring block concepts and to the workbench/substrate MCP context, including an explicit caveat about the reliability of self-generated tooling.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on a hierarchical block-selection gesture specification and asserting that recurring block concepts, the workbench vault, and substrate MCP artifacts are self-generated and only partially functional.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's perspective on how block selection relates to broader block-based tooling and to flag the provisional nature of self-generated code.
